input.prompt: I have a .NET 8 app running directly on a Linux server with root access. I suspect the high CPU is partly in native code (a native interop library). How do I get native call stacks in my trace?

I have a .NET 8 app running directly on a Linux server with root access. I suspect the high CPU is partly in native code (a native interop library). How do I get native call stacks in my trace?

input.initialPrompt: I have a .NET 8 app running directly on a Linux server with root access. I suspect the high CPU is partly in native code (a native interop library). How do I get native call stacks in my trace?

data.content:
For .NET 8 on Linux with native interop, use `dotnet-trace` with the **`perf`** or **EventPipe** approach that includes native frames:

**Option 1: `perf` (recommended for native stacks)**
```bash
# Record with perf (captures both managed + native frames)
perf record -g -p <PID>

# Generate managed symbol map so perf can resolve .NET frames
dotnet tool install -g dotnet-symbol
export DOTNET_PerfMapEnabled=1   # set before app starts
# Or for a running app, use crossgen2 to emit perf maps

perf report
```

**Option 2: `dotnet-trace` with native frames (.NET 8+)**
```bash
dotnet-trace collect -p <PID> --profile cpu-sampling
```
By default this only captures managed frames. To include native:

```bash
# Use the EventPipe provider with native callstacks enabled
dotnet-trace collect -p <PID> \
  --providers Microsoft-DotNETCore-SampleProfiler:0xF00000000:5
```

**Best approach for your case — `perf`:**
```bash
# 1. Set env vars BEFORE starting the app:
export DOTNET_PerfMapEnabled=1
export DOTNET_EnableWriteXorExecute=0

# 2. Run your app, then record:
perf record -g -p $(pidof yourapp) -- sleep 30

# 3. Analyze:
perf report --no-children
```

`DOTNET_PerfMapEnabled=1` makes the runtime emit `/tmp/perf-<pid>.map` so `perf` can resolve JIT-compiled managed method names alongside native frames from your interop library.

If you can't restart the app, you can also use **`perfcollect`** (Microsoft's wrapper script) which automates symbol resolution:

```bash
curl -OL https://aka.ms/perfcollect && chmod +x perfcollect
./perfcollect collect mytrace -pid <PID>
```
data.reasoningText:
